parent_goal_name: Albertans can meet their basic needs and live with financial stability
parent_service: Get income, health and other benefits for a severe disability
blurb: Income support, health benefits, and employment pathways for Albertans with severe disabilities — including AISH and the new Alberta Disability Assistance Program (ADAP).

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: Eligible Albertans receive the right disability income support
goals.description: Ensure people with severe disabilities can apply for the income support program that best fits their situation.

foundational_services.name: Adjudicate benefit decisions and eligibility
foundational_services.desc: Caseworkers assess medical, financial, and work capacity information to determine program placement and benefit levels

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

foundational_services.name: Conduct eligibility reviews of existing cases
foundational_services.desc: Periodic reassessment of active recipients to confirm continued eligibility based on medical condition, income, assets, and living arrangements

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

supporting.name: Check your eligibility for AISH or ADAP
supporting.desc: Determine eligibility based on disability severity, work capacity, financial situation, and Alberta residency. A single assessment determines whether AISH or ADAP is the right fit.
supporting.users: Persons with severe disabilities, potential applicants

supporting.name: Apply online through MyAlbertaSupports
supporting.desc: Submit a combined AISH/ADAP application online. You'll need your Alberta.ca account, medical documentation from your doctor, financial information, and proof of residency.

supporting.name: Apply by paper or with help
supporting.desc: Complete a paper application form, or get help from an Alberta Supports Centre or by phone. Staff can assist with the application process.
supporting.users: Applicants needing assistance or without internet

supporting.name: Submit medical documentation
supporting.desc: Your doctor completes the medical assessment confirming your condition is severe and permanent (AISH) or significantly limits work capacity (ADAP). The form documents diagnosis, treatment history, and prognosis.

supporting.name: Request rapid reinstatement
supporting.desc: If you left AISH within the last 24 months, you can request fast-tracked reinstatement without a full reapplication — provided the original eligibility criteria are still met.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Recipients can manage their benefits and meet ongoing obligations
goals.description: Support recipients in managing their ongoing benefits, reporting changes, requesting additional support, and handling account administration.

foundational_services.name: Process monthly payments
foundational_services.desc: Calculate and issue monthly income payments, child benefits, and personal benefits to all active recipients approximately four business days before the first of each month

foundational_services.name: Process income and asset assessments
foundational_services.desc: Evaluate employment income, other income sources, and assets against exemption thresholds (AISH: $1,072/month employment exemption; ADAP: $700/month) to calculate monthly benefit amounts

supporting.name: Report a change in your circumstances
supporting.desc: You must report changes as soon as they happen: income, employment, living arrangements, medical condition, family situation, or assets. Late reporting can cause overpayments you'll need to repay.

supporting.name: Submit your income statement
supporting.desc: You must report your income regularly to ensure your benefits are calculated correctly. Employment income, other income, and assets are assessed against exemption thresholds. Failure to report can result in overpayments that must be repaid.

supporting.name: Set up or change your direct deposit
supporting.desc: Arrange for your monthly benefit payments to be deposited directly into your bank account, or update your banking information when it changes.

supporting.name: Authorize a third party to act on your behalf
supporting.desc: Designate a personal representative, financial administrator, or trustee. You can also authorize third-party payment (e.g., rent to landlord), consent to release information, or enroll in the Informal Benefits Administration Program.

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

supporting.name: Get a replacement health benefits card
supporting.desc: Request a new Alberta Adult Health Benefit card if yours is lost, stolen, or damaged. The card provides coverage for prescription drugs, dental, optical, ambulance, and medical supplies.

supporting.name: Request a special diet benefit
supporting.desc: If your disability requires a medically prescribed special diet (diabetic, gluten-free, renal, etc.), apply for a monthly supplement of $26–$132 depending on diet type. Multiple diets can be combined up to policy maximums.

supporting.name: Request medical equipment or supplies
supporting.desc: Apply for coverage of disability-related medical equipment (wheelchairs, power scooters, CPAP devices — up to $2,351/item) or ongoing medical supplies (up to $473/month). Includes maintenance and repair of approved equipment.

supporting.name: Request travel benefits
supporting.desc: Apply for travel cost coverage for health-related travel ($0.37/km), court appearances, or approved training. Includes food ($26/day) and accommodation at actual cost when overnight travel is required.
supporting.users: Recipients who need to travel for health or program reasons

supporting.name: Request a specialized clothing or service animal benefit
supporting.desc: Apply for a specialized clothing benefit (up to $354/year) if your disability causes unusual wear or requires adapted clothing, or a service animal benefit ($63/month) for a certified service dog.
supporting.users: Recipients with clothing or service animal needs

supporting.name: Request a moving benefit
supporting.desc: Apply for coverage of moving costs when relocating due to an adapted residence need, escaping abuse, employment, or health and safety reasons. Assessed case by case.

supporting.name: Request other personal benefits
supporting.desc: Apply for additional support not covered by other categories: prenatal needs ($117/month from 14 weeks pregnant), infant care ($296 one-time per infant), remote community benefit ($48/month), medical alert service (actual cost), or other unusual needs assessed case by case.

supporting.name: Claim funeral benefits
supporting.desc: Survivors of a deceased AISH client can apply for funeral benefits: burial or cremation (up to $4,601), ceremony costs (up to $1,041), and related expenses. Processed through the Health and Funeral Benefit Unit.
supporting.users: Family members or estate representatives of deceased clients

main_services.name: Request a review of your benefits

supporting.name: Request reassessment between AISH and ADAP
supporting.desc: If your work capacity or medical condition changes, request reassessment to move between AISH and ADAP. During the transition period (July 2026 – December 2027), ADAP clients can request AISH reassessment.

supporting.name: Appeal a decision about your eligibility or benefits
supporting.desc: If you disagree with an AISH or ADAP decision, you have 30 days to submit a Notice of Appeal. The process moves through administrative review, then an Appeal Panel hearing. A further appeal to the Court of King's Bench (judicial review) is available within 30 days of the panel decision.
supporting.users: Applicants or recipients who disagree with a decision

channels.label: Appeals Secretariat offices (Edmonton, Calgary, Red Deer, Lethbridge)

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

supporting.name: Get independent advocacy for your disability rights
supporting.desc: The Office of the Disability Advocate and the Alberta Ombudsman can help if you have concerns about how your case was handled or need support navigating the system.
supporting.users: Recipients with complaints or needing advocacy

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: ADAP recipients are supported toward greater independence through employment
goals.description: Enable ADAP recipients to pursue employment opportunities with appropriate supports and income exemptions.

supporting.name: Access disability-related employment supports
supporting.desc: ADAP recipients can access employment services, job training, and workplace supports designed for people with disabilities. The program encourages workforce participation with a $700/month employment income exemption.

foundational_services.name: Administer overpayments and debt recovery
foundational_services.desc: Assess overpayments caused by unreported changes or administrative error, notify clients, determine exemption eligibility, and manage recovery through benefit deductions or repayment agreements

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act

foundational_services.name: Index benefits annually
foundational_services.desc: Adjust benefit rates each year based on CPI changes to maintain purchasing power for recipients

foundational_services.name: Manage personal representative and trustee oversight
foundational_services.desc: Register, monitor, and oversee personal representatives, financial administrators, and trustees who manage benefits on behalf of clients, including compliance with reporting requirements

legislation.full_name: Assured Income for the Severely Handicapped Act
